title: Honey Chili Vegetable Balls

ingredients.title: For the Vegetable Balls
items: ¼ cup capsicum (bell pepper), finely chopped
¼ cup spring onions, finely chopped
½ cup paneer or tofu, crumbled (optional for extra richness)

ingredients.title: For the Honey Chili Sauce
items: 1 tsp red chili sauce (adjust to taste)
1 tsp rice vinegar or white vinegar
1 tsp cornflour mixed with 2 tbsp water (slurry)
1-2 red chilies, finely sliced (optional, for extra heat)

instruction.title: 1. Prepare the Vegetable Balls
steps: In a large mixing bowl, combine cabbage, carrot, capsicum, spring onions, and paneer/tofu.
Add cornflour, all-purpose flour, ginger-garlic paste, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Adjust the consistency by adding a little water if needed. The mixture should hold together when shaped.
Shape the mixture into small balls (about 1.5 inches in diameter).

instruction.title: 2. Fry the Vegetable Balls
steps: Heat oil in a deep pan over medium heat.
Deep fry the balls until golden brown and crispy.
Remove and drain on paper towels.

instruction.title: 3. Prepare the Honey Chili Sauce
steps: In a wok or skillet, heat sesame oil.
Sauté garlic, ginger, and red chilies until fragrant.
Add soy sauce, honey, tomato ketchup, red chili sauce, and vinegar. Stir to combine.
Bring to a gentle simmer and add the cornflour slurry to thicken the sauce.

instruction.title: 4. Coat the Balls
steps: Add the fried vegetable balls into the sauce and toss gently to coat evenly.
Cook for another 1-2 minutes on low heat to let the flavors infuse.

instruction.title: 5. Garnish and Serve
steps: Sprinkle with chopped spring onions and toasted sesame seeds.
Serve hot as an appetizer or as part of a main meal with fried rice or noodles.

chefTips: Crunch Factor: Squeeze out excess water from vegetables for crispier balls.
Even Frying: Keep oil at medium heat to ensure balls cook through without burning.
Sauce Consistency: Use a cornstarch slurry to get that glossy, clingy sauce just like in restaurants.
Flavor Balance: Honey for sweetness, soy sauce for umami, vinegar for tang, and chili for heat – adjust to taste.
